title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Automation and Data Cl… · Language Clinic: Bounded Response Reads
summary: A climate archive streams large compressed files and uploads generated reports while bounding bytes, decompression, parsing, and temporary storage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py bounded response reads increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
